title: Decision Record
description: Schema mirror of the Notion decision-registry row defined in AGENT_PRODUCT_CYCLE.md §16 lines 2000-2024. Notion is the authoritative store; this schema enables client-side validation by the CEO agent, PR Anti-Drift gate, and manager agents before writing via the Notion MCP. Records are append-only — they are never deleted, only superseded by a new record that references this one in `superseded_by`.

decision_id.description: Auto-generated UUID. Referenced by related_decisions and superseded_by on other records.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the decision was made.

product.description: Which product repo this decision affects, or 'brain' for platform-level decisions.

layer.description: Which layer of the system made or owned the decision.

decision_type.description: Decision category. 'override' covers manual overrides and kill-switch activations.

context.description: What was happening when this decision was made.

rationale.description: Why this option was chosen over the alternatives.

alternatives.description: Required if any alternatives were considered. Empty array allowed when no alternatives existed.

confidence.description: Decision-maker's confidence in the decision at time of recording.

downstream_impact.description: What this decision affects going forward.

related_decisions.description: decision_ids this builds on or supersedes.

status.description: Lifecycle state. Records are never deleted — only superseded.

superseded_by.description: decision_id of the later decision that reversed or replaced this one. Required when status=superseded.
